concerns.text: Task T017 (metrics.py) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the output of T013 (solver.py) and T014 (warp.py) which generate the 3D points and warped frames required for metric calculation. A task consuming artifacts from US2 cannot be parallel-safe relative to US2 implementation tasks. The [P] tag is semantically incorrect here.

Task T020 (main.py orchestrator) is placed in Phase 5 (US3) but logically depends on the completion of T008-T016 (US1 & US2) to chain the pipeline. While the 'Dependencies' section notes this, the task placement in Phase 5 implies it runs after US3 metrics are ready, creating a circular dependency or a 'consumer before producer' risk if T020 is intended to run the full pipeline. T020 should be a post-US3 integration task or explicitly sequenced after T016.

Task T011 and T012 are listed as separate tasks within US1 but describe edge-case handling logic that is inherently part of T010 (extract_features.py). While not a hard ordering violation, splitting them as independent tasks with no explicit 'depends on T010' marker risks a developer attempting to implement T011/T012 before T010 is complete, leading to missing context. Consider merging or adding explicit 'Depends on T010' markers.
